pattern: Using the array as its own presence map, mark the slot each value points to; any slot left unmarked names a value that never appeared, found in linear time with no extra storage.
hints: Every value lies in 1..n, so each one corresponds to a slot in the same array that you can stamp as visited.
For each value v, mark index v-1 (for example by negating it); afterward the still-unmarked indices reveal the missing numbers.
Pass once negating nums[abs(v)-1] for each v, then pass again collecting i+1 for every index i whose value is still positive.

edge_inputs.description: several numbers missing from the range
length two with one missing value
single element equal to one, nothing missing
complete permutation has no missing numbers
all duplicates of one value leave many missing
missing values at both ends of the range
only the largest value is missing

stress.description: values drawn uniformly from 1..n so a varying set of numbers never appears
stress.note: uniform draws from 1..n leave a random, well-distributed set of absent values
